rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER CALVI VS. VITO J. LBERATI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Strike Claim For Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Renee C. St. Clair In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 24, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Strike Claim For Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint. As a preliminary matter, the Court vacates its March 13, 2015 Order granting Defendant's motion as unopposed. Apparently Defendant filed its motion twice with two different hearing dates. While Plaintiff did not file an opposition to the first motion, Plaintiff did file an opposition to this subsequently filed motion. Accordingly, the Court vacates its previous order in favor of ruling on the merits of the motion. Defendant's renewed motion to strike pursuant to CCP sec. 1008(b) is denied. The prayer for relief in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") only seeks punitive damages under the third cause of action for Willful and Capricious Failure to Pay Maintenance, Found, and Cure. (See SAC, p. 9, par. 5; SAC, p. 7, pars. 26-27.) Per Atlantic Sounding Co., Inc. v. Townsend (2009) 557 U.S. 404, 424-25, punitive damages are available for the willful and wanton disregard of the maintenance and cure obligation. Thus, the motion to strike paragraph 5 from the prayer for relief is denied. Defendant also seeks to strike references to reckless behavior under the first and second causes of action. The motion to strike this language is denied. Because the prayer for relief only seeks punitive damages under the third cause of action, striking the allegations of recklessness from the first two causes of action would serve no practical purpose. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL RODRIGUEZ VS. LARRY FLYNT'S HUSTLER CLUB SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Terminate Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Â§ 1281.2(C); Motion To Bifurcate Case Against Defendant Penthouse Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Â§ 1048(B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 24, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SAMUEL RODRIGUEZ'S Motion To Terminate Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 1281.2(C); Motion To Bifurcate Case Against Defendant Penthouse Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 1048(B). Plaintiff's motion to terminate the arbitration and alternative motion to bifurcate the case is denied and SAW's motion for sanctions is granted each of plaintiff's two attorneys but not their client are required to pay a penalty of $250 to the court no later than May 29, 2015. The motion to terminate the arbitration and alternative motion to bifurcate the case is an impermissible and unacknowledged effort to seek reconsideration of prior rulings without complying with any of the requirements of the reconsideration statute and, therefore, the legal contentions therein run afoul of the certification requirement of section 128.7(b)(2). Effective deterrence of repeat conduct of this nature is best achieved by a monetary penalty against plaintiff's attorneys rather than any payment to SAW. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS F MCCORMICK VS. SECURITY MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 24, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT SECURITY MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One. Denied. Interrogatory 18 and document request 10 are overbroad and not narrowly tailored to meet the standard of discovery relevance, much less the more stringent standard of direct relevance that applies to requests seeking information protected by plaintiff's right of privacy. Defendant does not explain what information it seeks by these requests that it cannot obtain through less invasive requests. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MIEKO SAITO VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 24, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Complaint. Granted with twenty days leave to amend. None of the claims alleged in the complaint are based on a statute, as required by Government Code section 815. While it appears that plaintiff may not be able to allege any claims for the conduct for which she seeks relief that are based on a statute and for which she satisfied the claims presentation requirement of the Government Code and are not time-barred, at this early stage of the litigation, plaintiff should be given the opportunity to attempt to do so.
